prompt: A long, dimly lit stone corridor with iron hooks lining both walls, stretching deep into the distance in perfect one-point perspective. On each hook hangs a wedding dress. CRITICAL — the size progression must be visually unmistakable: The FIRST dress on the left (foreground) is a full-length adult bridal gown, floor-sweeping, heavy ivory fabric, reaching the ground. Each successive dress moving deeper into the corridor is NOTICEABLY smaller than the one before it. By the middle of the corridor, the dresses reach only knee height. The last dress, hanging directly under a single harsh overhead cage light at the far vanishing point, is tiny — no larger than a child's doll dress, perhaps 30cm long — heartbreakingly small, perfectly centered and spotlit. The size contrast between the first dress and the last must be immediately legible to any viewer without explanation. The scale difference should feel shocking and wrong. Cold stone walls with peeling plaster, dirty floor, no people, complete silence. Hyper-realistic photographic style, cinematic wide-angle lens, deep shadows with chiaroscuro lighting, desaturated ivory and grey palette. Negative prompt: no people, no text, no numbers, no labels, no charts, no graphs, no similarly-sized dresses, no uniform sizing — the progression from large to tiny must be dramatic and unmistakable
insight: 12 million girls are married before age 18 every year — 33,000 every single day — the majority before they turn 15.
